generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental sensor capturing real-time temperature data with high-frequency sampling for critical HVAC performance metrics. Embedded in BIM with spatial coordinates and calibration parameters."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Environmental sensor capturing real-time humidity data with geometric alignment to physical building layout in BIM. Supports dynamic operational data acquisition."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalHumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance OccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Occupancy sensor monitoring real-time space utilization with spatial mapping in BIM. Part of the heterogeneous sensing infrastructure for building control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance EquipmentStatusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "System health sensor monitoring equipment operational status (e.g., HVAC components) with real-time synchronization capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EquipmentStatusDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Energy consumption sensor capturing real-time power usage data with bidirectional synchronization to DT for operational optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalHumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalHumiditySensor
]

instance OccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupancySensor
]

instance EquipmentStatusDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EquipmentStatusSensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BuildingGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static geometric representation derived from BIM containing spatial layouts, components, and sensor/actuator placement coordinates."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimToDigitalTwinTransformationEnabler
]

instance BuildingBehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic behavioral model encapsulating control logic for Building Control Systems (BCS) components including sensors, actuators, and controllers."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimToDigitalTwinTransformationEnabler
]

instance BuildingAnalyticalModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model generated via Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles for automated transformation into deployable DT artifacts (e.g., simulation models)."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimToDigitalTwinTransformationEnabler
]

instance RealTimeOperationalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuously synchronized dynamic data including sensor readings, actuator feedback, and environmental parameters from physical building."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted, EnvironmentalHumidityDataTransmitted, OccupancyDataTransmitted, EquipmentStatusDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance StaticMetadata : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Static metadata including calibration parameters, spatial coordinates, and device specifications embedded in BIM models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData StaticMetadataDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BimToDigitalTwinTransformationEnabler
]

instance StaticMetadataDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EquipmentStatusSensor, EnergyConsumptionSensor
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "IoT data ingestion and edge-to-cloud connectivity solution ensuring scalable, protocol-agnostic communication between physical sensors and DT."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataIngestionService, SensorDataSynchronizationService
]

instance EclipseDittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital twin framework for modeling, managing, and synchronizing the DT's state with real-time data streams from Eclipse Hono."
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalTwinStateManagementService, BidirectionalSynchronizationService
]

instance UnrealEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "High-fidelity visualization and simulation platform enabling immersive 3D representations of physical assets and dynamic behaviors."
    DTDFVocab:enables ImmersiveVisualizationService, InteractiveSimulationService
]

instance BimToDigitalTwinTransformationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) framework automating extraction of structured representations from enriched BIM models into deployable DT artifacts."
    DTDFVocab:enables AutomatedModelTransformationService, DeviceModelDeploymentService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeDataIngestionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service handling continuous, low-latency ingestion of sensor data from heterogeneous networks into the DT framework."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeDataInsight
]

instance SensorDataSynchronizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service ensuring bidirectional synchronization between physical sensor states and digital twin representations."
    DTDFVocab:provides SynchronizedDataInsight
]

instance DigitalTwinStateManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing the current state of the digital twin by processing and validating incoming data streams."
    DTDFVocab:provides DigitalTwinStateInsight
]

instance BidirectionalSynchronizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service facilitating event-driven updates and periodic synchronization between physical and virtual domains."
    DTDFVocab:provides SynchronizationStatusInsight
]

instance AutomatedModelTransformationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service automating the conversion of BIM models into executable digital twin components using MDE principles."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelTransformationInsight
]

instance DeviceModelDeploymentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service deploying device models (sensors/actuators) into the runtime environment with standardized configurations."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeploymentStatusInsight
]

instance ImmersiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing 3D BIM overlays with real-time data visualization for Facility Managers."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizedDataInsight
]

instance InteractiveSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling virtual experiments and scenario testing within the digital twin environment."
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationResultInsight
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing continuous observation of physical asset states (e.g., structural integrity, environmental conditions)."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating forecasts (e.g., equipment failure, energy trends) using runtime data for proactive decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveForecastInsight
]

instance ControlAndOperationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling remote/automated control of physical systems (e.g., HVAC, lighting) via digital command center."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAction
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service performing lifecycle optimization of infrastructure performance, cost, and sustainability through data-driven adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing current sensor readings and environmental parameters with low-latency updates from physical infrastructure."
]

instance SynchronizedDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight confirming bidirectional synchronization status between physical sensors and digital twin representations."
]

instance DigitalTwinStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight representing the current state of the digital twin after processing all synchronized data streams."
]

instance SynchronizationStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight indicating the health and performance of the synchronization mechanism between physical and virtual domains."
]

instance ModelTransformationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight confirming successful transformation of BIM models into executable digital twin components."
]

instance DeploymentStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing status of deployed device models in the runtime environment."
]

instance VisualizedDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight showing immersive 3D visualization of building with real-time sensor data overlays."
]

instance SimulationResultInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight containing results from virtual experiments and scenario simulations within the digital twin."
]

instance SystemStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight tracking continuous device states (sensor readings, actuator statuses) for operational monitoring."
]

instance OperationalAnomalyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight identifying deviations in real-time data indicating potential operational anomalies."
]

instance PredictiveForecastInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight containing forecasts for equipment failure, energy consumption trends, and other predictive analytics."
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing data-driven recommendations for lifecycle optimization of building performance."
]

instance ControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action enabling remote adjustment of physical systems (e.g., HVAC setpoints, lighting controls) via digital twin interface."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance HvacControllerActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "HVAC system controller executing commands derived from digital twin's control logic for temperature and humidity regulation."
]

instance LightingControlActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Lighting control unit adjusting illumination based on occupancy and environmental conditions from digital twin insights."
]

instance DamperActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "HVAC damper actuator controlling airflow distribution with constraints encoded in digital twin's behavioral models."
]

instance ValveActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Valve actuator managing fluid flow with operational boundaries defined in digital twin for safety compliance."
]

instance MotorizedBlindsActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Motorized blind actuator adjusting window coverage based on solar gain predictions from digital twin analytics."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT system enables **bidirectional control flows** from the digital twin to physical infrastructure, allowing Facility Managers to interact with and operate the physical building via the DT interface. Control commands are transmitted through the **central DT Framework**, which facilitates real-time monitoring and adjustments. While the document does not specify exact command types (e.g., HVAC setpoints, lighting adjustments) or technical mechanisms (e.g., conflict resolution, authentication), the system supports **runtime configuration management**, enabling dynamic adjustments without full redeployment. The framework’s modular design allows for **custom service integration**, which may include control logic validation and feedback loops to ensure command execution aligns with operational goals. The emphasis is on **interactive visualization** for immersive monitoring and control, bridging the gap between static BIM representations and dynamic operational needs."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure **continuous synchronization** between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, enabling near-instantaneous bidirectional updates. While explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, min) are not specified, the framework prioritizes **low-latency operational requirements** for real-time monitoring and control, particularly for *Facility Managers* interacting with the deployed Digital Twin. The system supports **dynamic operational needs** of the AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) domain, implying adaptive time scales for different services (e.g., device modeling, analytics, or control adjustments). Runtime configuration management further enables **on-the-fly adjustments** without redeployment, suggesting a flexible temporal granularity aligned with evolving operational demands. The absence of hard-coded latency thresholds implies a focus on **functional responsiveness** rather than rigid temporal constraints, though interoperability and scalability requirements (NF_X) may indirectly enforce performance bounds for heterogeneous system integration."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **hybrid orchestration pattern** combining **centralized coordination** (via the *DT Framework* hub) with **modular decentralization** (through standardized extension points and runtime adaptability). Key architectural components include:
  - **Central Hub (*DT Framework*)**: Acts as a **unified orchestrator** for bidirectional data exchange between enriched BIM models and operational Digital Twins, managing the **life cycle** of the twin (e.g., deployment, monitoring, control).
  - **Role-Specific Workflows**: Orchestration is role-driven, with *Building Control Engineers* initiating device modeling, *Facility Managers* handling real-time operations, and *DT Engineers* extending capabilities via service integration. This **divide-and-conquer approach** ensures specialized resource management (e.g., computational, data) aligned with operational needs.
  - **Modular Service Integration**: The framework’s **extension points** enable **plug-and-play analytics or control services**, with orchestration handled via runtime configuration management to avoid redeployment overhead. This supports **dynamic resource allocation** and **elastic scaling** (as implied by NF_X scalability requirements).
  - **Interoperability Layer**: The system’s **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** emphasize **heterogeneous system integration**, suggesting a **service-oriented architecture (SOA)** with standardized protocols for component communication. The absence of explicit middleware details implies reliance on **industry-standard interoperability frameworks** (e.g., OPC UA, REST APIs) for cross-system coordination."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **runtime operation**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** within the BIM environment, enriching it with semantic representations of Building Control System (BCS) components (e.g., functional requirements **F_X** in Table III). This phase establishes a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations.
  2. **Model Transformation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts structured representations from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention. This ensures seamless transition from static BIM to dynamic DT representations.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed, enabling real-time synchronization between the physical building and its digital twin. This phase supports **interactive visualization** for immersive monitoring and control, aligning with Facility Manager use cases (Table I).
  4. **Runtime Operation & Evolution**: The DT operates continuously, with **real-time data exchange** ensuring synchronization. The modular architecture, featuring **standardized extension points**, allows DT Engineers to integrate **custom services and analytics** (e.g., plugins) to adapt to evolving operational needs. Governance is implicit through the framework’s adherence to structured use cases (Table II) and stakeholder-specific roles (Table I)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs the **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric** to quantify how well the resulting DT reflects its physical counterpart, serving as a **fidelity validation mechanism**. The framework ensures **geometric fidelity** through BIM’s static representations and **behavioral fidelity** via automated extraction of domain semantics (e.g., sensor/actuator configurations) and runtime synchronization. However, the document does not detail **specific validation methods** (e.g., automated anomaly detection, outlier correction) or **uncertainty sources/quantification** (e.g., data latency, sensor drift). While the system validates **configuration and runtime data**, the exact processes for **error detection/correction** or **quality assurance** are not specified. The focus is on **automated model generation** and **real-time synchronization** rather than granular fidelity management."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT system employs a **central hub architecture** with a **modular, service-oriented design**, supported by technologies like **Eclipse Hono** (for IoT data ingestion) and **Eclipse Ditto** (for digital twin state management). This architecture enables **bidirectional data exchange** between physical and digital components, ensuring synchronized operational states. The framework’s **standardized extension points** allow for seamless integration of custom services and analytics, while its **runtime configuration management** enhances adaptability. Network communication is optimized for **real-time synchronization**, though specific protocols (e.g., MQTT, HTTP/2), topology (e.g., star, mesh), or bandwidth/latency requirements are not detailed. The system prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** and **scalability** from individual components to entire infrastructure systems, but explicit reliability (e.g., redundancy), security (e.g., encryption, access control), or network topology details are not provided in the document."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT system is deployed as a **centralized framework** acting as a hub for bidirectional data and control flows, supporting the entire building lifecycle from BIM-based device modeling to real-time operational management. Deployment is initiated by **Building Control Engineers** using BIM tools, with **Facility Managers** interacting with the deployed DT for monitoring and control, and **DT Engineers** extending capabilities via service integration. The framework emphasizes **runtime configuration management**, allowing adjustments without full redeployment, which implies support for **dynamic scaling** and **low-overhead updates**. While the document does not specify hosting platforms (e.g., cloud, edge, on-premise), computational requirements, or redundancy mechanisms, the **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points** suggest flexibility in deployment models. The system’s focus on **accessibility for varying technical expertise** and **maintainability** aligns with a scalable, user-centric design, though explicit details on hosting infrastructure or availability/reliability assurances are absent."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates horizontally with **external systems** primarily through:
  - **BIM Tools as the Semantic Backbone**: Enriched BIM models (e.g., Revit, ArchiCAD) serve as the foundational data source for device modeling and twin deployment, with **bidirectional synchronization** enabled via standardized BIM export/import formats (e.g., IFC, though not explicitly named).
  - **Real-Time Data Exchange Protocols**: The framework leverages **Eclipse Hono** (for IoT telemetry) and **Eclipse Ditto** (for digital twin state management) to facilitate low-latency, protocol-agnostic data streaming between physical sensors, enterprise systems, and the DT. Specific integration protocols (e.g., BACnet, Modbus, OPC UA) are **not mentioned**, but the emphasis on **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** implies compatibility with industry-standard IoT/building automation protocols.
  - **Enterprise System Dependencies**: The system’s **modular architecture** supports integration with external enterprise tools (e.g., CMMS, ERP) via **standardized extension points**, though no explicit APIs or middleware (e.g., REST/gRPC) are detailed. The **DT Framework’s central hub** acts as a translator between proprietary BIM formats and operational data schemas, ensuring accessibility for users with varying technical expertise.
  - **Limited Cross-DT Coordination**: While the framework enables **scalable deployment**, there is no evidence of **federated Digital Twin networks** or cross-system orchestration (e.g., multi-building constellations). Integration with other Digital Twins or enterprise-wide systems is implied but not technically specified beyond BIM tool dependencies."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The framework adheres to **BIM and AECO-specific standards** to ensure interoperability and semantic consistency across the Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle. It is explicitly designed to be **compatible with existing Building Information Modeling (BIM) systems and standards**, addressing the gap between BIM’s static representations and the dynamic requirements of DTs. The framework introduces a **modeling methodology to standardize BIM semantics**, creating a **transformation pipeline** that maps enriched BIM models (e.g., those incorporating Building Control Systems (BCS) components) into deployable DT artifacts. This standardization effort ensures that **domain semantics** (e.g., sensor types, control logic) are consistently represented and transformed, enabling seamless integration with **AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) workflows**. While the document does not specify exact certification requirements or additional interoperability standards (e.g., IFC, OPC UA), the emphasis on **BIM compatibility** and **semantic standardization** implies alignment with broader industry efforts to unify digital twins with existing infrastructure. The framework’s modular architecture further supports **extension via standardized interfaces**, facilitating compliance with emerging DT standards as they evolve."
]
